title: alnus glutinosa var. oxyacanthifolia
intro: Alnus glutinosa var. oxyacanthifolia, also known as the Alder Buckthorn or Alder Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its high tannin content. The plant is known to provide several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic to combat infections. Herbal preparations of Alnus glutinosa var. oxyacanthifolia include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical applications for various health ailments.
